prompt: Implement cursor-based pagination for the API using TDD. It should support forward/backward navigation with opaque cursors encoding the last seen ID.

turns.content:
<task>
Implement cursor-based pagination for the API using TDD. It should support forward/backward navigation with opaque cursors encoding the last seen ID.
</task>

arguments.summary: Project has api/ and tests/ directories. Will implement cursor pagination with TDD in api/pagination.py.

arguments.content:
import pytest
from api.pagination import CursorPaginator, encode_cursor, decode_cursor


def test_encode_decode_cursor():
    cursor = encode_cursor(42)
    assert isinstance(cursor, str)
    assert decode_cursor(cursor) == 42


def test_decode_invalid_cursor():
    assert decode_cursor('invalid!!!') is None
    assert decode_cursor('') is None


def test_first_page():
    items = [{'id': i, 'name': f'item_{i}'} for i in range(1, 21)]
    paginator = CursorPaginator(items, page_size=5)
    page = paginator.get_page()
    assert len(page.items) == 5
    assert page.items[0]['id'] == 1
    assert page.items[-1]['id'] == 5
    assert page.next_cursor is not None
    assert page.prev_cursor is None  # first page


def test_second_page_with_cursor():
    items = [{'id': i, 'name': f'item_{i}'} for i in range(1, 21)]
    paginator = CursorPaginator(items, page_size=5)
    first = paginator.get_page()
    second = paginator.get_page(after=first.next_cursor)
    assert len(second.items) == 5
    assert second.items[0]['id'] == 6
    assert second.items[-1]['id'] == 10
    assert second.prev_cursor is not None


def test_last_page():
    items = [{'id': i, 'name': f'item_{i}'} for i in range(1, 8)]
    paginator = CursorPaginator(items, page_size=5)
    first = paginator.get_page()
    second = paginator.get_page(after=first.next_cursor)
    assert len(second.items) == 2  # only 2 left
    assert second.next_cursor is None  # no more pages


def test_backward_navigation():
    items = [{'id': i, 'name': f'item_{i}'} for i in range(1, 21)]
    paginator = CursorPaginator(items, page_size=5)
    first = paginator.get_page()
    second = paginator.get_page(after=first.next_cursor)
    back = paginator.get_page(before=second.prev_cursor)
    assert back.items[0]['id'] == 1
    assert len(back.items) == 5


def test_empty_items():
    paginator = CursorPaginator([], page_size=5)
    page = paginator.get_page()
    assert page.items == []
    assert page.next_cursor is None
    assert page.prev_cursor is None

arguments.summary: All 7 tests pass. CursorPaginator uses base64-encoded opaque cursors. Forward nav with after= finds items with id > cursor id. Backward nav with before= finds page_size items before the cursor. First page has no prev_cursor, last page has no next_cursor.
arguments.final_answer:
Implemented cursor-based pagination with TDD:
- api/pagination.py: encode_cursor/decode_cursor use base64 opaque strings. CursorPaginator.get_page(after=, before=) supports forward and backward navigation. Page dataclass holds items, next_cursor, prev_cursor.
- tests/test_pagination.py: 7 tests covering cursor encoding, invalid cursors, first/second/last pages, backward navigation, and empty input.
All 7 tests pass.
